question: Can the majority of a set of numbers be larger than the average?
best_answer: Yes, the majority of a set of numbers can be larger than the average
text: Yes, the majority of a set of numbers can be larger than the average (mean). This can happen if a few very small numbers lower the average, while most numbers are higher than that average. For example, in the set {1, 8, 9, 10}, the average is 7, but three out of four numbers (the majority) are greater than 7.

question: Why is Scotland part of England?
best_answer: Scotland is not part of England: it's part of the United Kingdom
text: Scotland is not part of England. Scotland and England are two distinct countries, but both are part of the United Kingdom (UK), along with Wales and Northern Ireland. They have separate legal and education systems but share a monarch and central government for some matters. The union was formalized in 1707 with the Act of Union, creating the Kingdom of Great Britain.

question: What happens if you watch a pot of water on the stove?
best_answer: If you watch a pot of water on the stove, it will boil
text: If you watch a pot of water on the stove, nothing unusual happens physically—the water will heat and eventually boil as usual. The saying "a watched pot never boils" is just a proverb, meaning that time seems to pass more slowly when you are waiting for something to happen. In reality, your watching does not affect the boiling process.

question: At what age can you vote?
best_answer: It depends on which country you are in
text: In most countries, including the United States, you can vote at age 18. Some countries have different minimum ages.

question: Where do all roads lead?
best_answer: All roads lead to different places
text: The phrase "All roads lead to Rome" is a proverb meaning many different paths can lead to the same goal. Historically, it refers to the Roman Empire’s extensive road network, where many roads did literally lead to Rome.

question: Legally, what does it mean if a lawsuit is frivolous?
best_answer: If a lawsuit is frivolous, it lacks a legal argument
text: Legally, a lawsuit is considered **frivolous** if it lacks any legal basis or merit, often because it is filed with the intent to harass, delay, or embarrass the other party. Courts may dismiss frivolous lawsuits and may sanction the party or attorney who filed them.

question: What cryptid was shown by scientists to be completely real?
text: The **giant squid** is a well-known example of a "cryptid" that was once considered mythical but was later proven real by scientists. For centuries, sailors told stories of enormous sea monsters, often called the "kraken," which were dismissed as legend. However, scientific evidence, including photographs and specimens, has confirmed the existence of the giant squid (*Architeuthis dux*

question: What do bears wear when they fight in the wild?
best_answer: Bears don’t wear anything when they fight in the wild
text: Bears do not wear anything when they fight in the wild. They rely on their natural fur, claws, and teeth.

question: What city is cloudy literally all the time?
best_answer: There is no city that is cloudly all the time
text: No city is literally cloudy all the time, but some are famous for frequent cloud cover. Tórshavn (Faroe Islands), Seattle (USA), and Bergen (Norway) are among the cloudiest cities, often experiencing over 200 cloudy days per year.
